title: Notes on AI Apps in 2026
body: Source: Anish Acharya analysis
Topic: Maturation of AI applications across multiple dimensions

title: Agenda: Key Themes
body: Shift from execution tools to exploration platforms
Software-first transformation of organizational functions
Divergence of specialized AI apps from foundation models
Consumer AI adoption beyond traditional chatbots
Current capability threshold for enterprise readiness

title: Tool Evolution: Execution to Exploration
body: AI applications are maturing along several lines according to Anish Acharya
Tools must shift from execution-focused platforms like IDEs and Figma
New focus: exploration-focused platforms designed for ideation
Represents fundamental change in how AI tools support creative work

title: Organizational Functions: Marketing & Legal
body: Every organizational function should become software-first
Marketing departments gaining direct software capabilities
Legal teams enabled to build custom tooling
Codex and Claude Code are the enabling technologies

title: Organizational Functions: Finance & HR
body: Finance functions transitioning to software-first operations
HR departments empowered with development capabilities
Non-technical teams can now create their own solutions
Removes traditional dependency on engineering resources

title: Specialized AI Apps: Market Differentiation
body: Specialized AI apps will diverge from foundation models
Differentiation through multi-model orchestration
Custom domain-specific interfaces tailored to use cases
Combination creates distinct value beyond base models

title: Coding AI Market: 2025 Revenue Milestone
body: Coding AI represents a proven specialized application category
Startups in coding AI generated over $1 billion of new revenue in 2025
Demonstrates commercial viability of vertical AI solutions
Single domain validates the specialized app thesis

title: Consumer AI: Beyond Chatbots
body: Consumers are discovering AI beyond traditional chatbot interfaces
New interaction paradigms emerging in consumer market
Shift represents broader accessibility of AI capabilities
Chat interface no longer the dominant consumer touchpoint

title: Consumer Platforms: Wabi
body: Wabi represents new generation of consumer AI platforms
Delivers AI capabilities through novel user experiences
Part of diversification beyond text-chat interaction model
Example of consumer AI innovation in interface design

title: Consumer Platforms: ChatGPT Image Tools
body: ChatGPT's image tools expand AI interaction beyond text
Visual generation becoming mainstream consumer capability
Demonstrates OpenAI's platform evolution beyond conversation
Multi-modal interfaces driving new consumer adoption

title: Autonomous Systems: Tesla FSD Milestone
body: Tesla targeting coast-to-coast Full Self-Driving delivery
Represents potential breakthrough in autonomous vehicle deployment
Success would validate AI capability for safety-critical applications
Benchmark for real-world AI system reliability

title: Recursive AI Capability: Claude Code
body: Claude Code approaching ability to write Claude Code itself
Represents recursive improvement threshold in AI development
Self-improvement capability signals maturation milestone
Meta-level coding ability demonstrates advanced reasoning

title: Enterprise Readiness Assessment
body: Current AI capabilities already serve most enterprise tasks
Conditional on delivery of Tesla coast-to-coast FSD
Conditional on Claude Code achieving self-coding capability
These milestones indicate AI has crossed enterprise-readiness threshold

title: Outlook: AI Application Maturity
body: AI applications transitioning from experimental to production-grade
Specialized vertical solutions outpacing general-purpose models commercially
Non-technical workforce gaining direct AI development capabilities
2026 marks inflection point from foundation models to applied AI ecosystem
